rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 11, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Six States Distributors, Inc.¿s amended motion to quash service of summons is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: LINE 2, Defendant Turner Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-5 and off calendar as to issues 6 and 7 (false representation and marketing of an asbestos product). Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if considered, the declarations of Ms. Bennett, Mr. Borja and Mr. Mautner merely create a triable issue when read in conjunction with plaintiffs¿ evidence. Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the development of Mr. Peterson¿s asbestos-related disease. Dr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson. Issues 6 and 7 are taken off calendar because plaintiffs represented they dismissed their false representation and strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
